root_text.an4.4:1.1: “Catūsu, bhikkhave, micchā paṭipajjamāno bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an4.4:1.3: Mātari, bhikkhave, micchā paṭipajjamāno bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an4.4:1.4: Pitari, bhikkhave, micchā paṭipajjamāno …pe… tathāgate, bhikkhave, micchā paṭipajjamāno …pe… tathāgatasāvake, bhikkhave, micchā paṭipajjamāno bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an4.4:1.5: Imesu kho, bhikkhave, catūsu micchā paṭipajjamāno bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an4.4:2.1: Catūsu, bhikkhave, sammā paṭipajjamāno paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an4.4:2.3: Mātari, bhikkhave, sammā paṭipajjamāno paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an4.4:2.4: Pitari, bhikkhave, sammā paṭipajjamāno …pe… tathāgate, bhikkhave, sammā paṭipajjamāno …pe… tathāgatasāvake, bhikkhave, sammā paṭipajjamāno paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an4.4:2.5: Imesu kho, bhikkhave, catūsu sammā paṭipajjamāno paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavatīti.

translation_text.an4.4:1.1: “When a foolish, incompetent untrue person acts wrongly toward four individuals they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an4.4:1.4: father … a Realized One … and a disciple of a Realized One.
translation_text.an4.4:1.5: When a foolish, incompetent untrue person acts wrongly toward these four individuals they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an4.4:2.1: When an astute, competent true person acts rightly toward four individuals they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.
translation_text.an4.4:2.4: father … a Realized One … and a disciple of a Realized One.
translation_text.an4.4:2.5: When an astute, competent true person acts rightly toward these four individuals they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.
translation_text.an4.4:3.3: or a Realized One, a Buddha,
translation_text.an4.4:4.3: they’re criticized in this life by the astute,
translation_text.an4.4:4.4: and they depart to be reborn in a place of loss.
translation_text.an4.4:5.3: or a Realized One, a Buddha,
translation_text.an4.4:6.3: they’re praised in this life by the astute,
translation_text.an4.4:6.4: and they depart to rejoice in heaven.”

variant_text.an4.4:4.1: Tāya naṁ adhammacariyāya → tāya adhammacariyāya (bj, sya-all, km); tāya adhammacāriyāya (pts1ed)
variant_text.an4.4:5.6: puññaṁ etādiso → puññampi tādiso (bj, km); puññaṁpi tādiso (sya-all)
variant_text.an4.4:6.4: sagge pamodatī”ti → sagge ca modatīti (bj); sagge pamodantīti (pts1ed)

reference_text.an4.4:1.1: cck21.5, dr21.5, ms15A4_18, msdiv4, ndp17.6, sya21.5, vri36.5
